name: set-isolation
description: Explains and flips one instance's version isolation on/off — whether its mods/saves/config live in its own versions/<name>/ or the shared .minecraft. Use when the user asks about shared vs isolated mods/saves or wants isolation toggled ("版本隔离","独立mod","共享mod","隔离设置").
body:
# Version isolation

`instance(action=set_isolation, instance, enable)` — omit `enable` to just report current
state without changing it.

- **ON** — this instance's `mods/`, `saves/`, `config/`, `resourcepacks/`,
  `shaderpacks/` live under its own `versions/<name>/`; changes affect only it.
- **OFF** — this instance FOLLOWS the global/parent preset's own directory setting
  instead of using its own `versions/<name>/` folder — it is NOT unconditionally
  "shared", it inherits whatever the current global default is. In the common case
  that default IS the shared base `.minecraft` (so content installed while off leaks
  into every other instance following that same default) — but check the actual
  current global setting rather than assuming; warn before switching off, and again
  before installing into an instance that is (or effectively is, via the global
  default) shared with others.
- A modpack-imported instance is always isolated regardless of this setting — never
  offer to disable it or call it shared.
- Flipping the switch doesn't move existing files between the two folders, only future
  writes.
- The currently-selected instance's isolation state is already shown to you automatically
  each turn (runtime context: version isolation). For OTHER instances, `game(action=list)` /
  `instance(action=list)` only show each instance's run directory path, from which isolation
  can be inferred (`versions/<name>/` vs. elsewhere) — there is no explicit isolation flag
  in that output.
